description: OmniMentor benchmark scenarios with gold labels for TPM architectural decision evaluation.

scenarios.title: Deploy Catalog API Schema Migration
scenarios.prompt: The Catalog Team needs to deploy a schema migration to the Catalog API that adds a new 'variants' field to product records. This change affects the CDC event schema consumed by Search Indexer. Identify the owner, map dependencies, assess blast radius, and determine safe deployment actions.
goldSafeActions: Update Search Indexer mapping before deploying Catalog API schema change
Coordinate with Search Platform Team on CDC event schema update
Verify Storefront BFF product-detail page renders correctly post-deploy
Monitor Search Indexer CDC lag — rollback if > 2 min
Schedule deployment outside bulk-import window
goldBlastRadius: Search Indexer ingestion failures if mapping not updated first
Stale search results on Storefront and Mobile BFF
Storefront product pages may break if BFF not updated for new field
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Correct owner is Catalog Team. Key risk is Search Indexer CDC schema mismatch — evidence art-cat-003 explicitly states mapping must be updated first. PIR-2026-018 (art-cat-005) provides historical precedent for indexer lag issues.

scenarios.title: Pricing Engine Degradation During Flash Sale
scenarios.prompt: During a flash sale event, the Pricing Engine is experiencing elevated latency (p99 > 3s, normal is 200ms). Active promotions are returning stale cached prices. Determine the responsible owner, map the dependency impact, assess blast radius, and recommend safe actions.
goldSafeActions: Engage Pricing Team on-call for latency investigation
Verify Promo Service is returning promotions within SLA
Confirm Catalog API fallback to cached base price is active
Alert Commerce Team that Cart prices may be stale
Consider temporarily disabling non-critical promo rules to reduce load
goldBlastRadius: Incorrect prices shown on Storefront via Catalog API fallback
Cart Service displaying stale prices to customers
Revenue impact if flash sale prices not applied correctly
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Pricing Team. Evidence art-cat-004 documents the synchronous dependency, fallback behavior (stale cached price for 15 min), and blast radius to Storefront and Cart.

scenarios.title: Deploy Checkout Orchestrator Saga Timeout Change
scenarios.prompt: Following PIR-2026-031, the Commerce Team wants to deploy a change to Checkout Orchestrator increasing the saga timeout from 5s to 8s and adding a circuit breaker on Inventory Sync calls. Identify the owner, map all dependencies, assess blast radius, and list safe deployment steps.
goldSafeActions: Coordinate with Payments Team before deploying changes touching checkout saga
Verify circuit breaker thresholds with Supply Chain Team (Inventory Sync owner)
Test saga compensating actions (void authorization, cancel reservation) in staging
Monitor checkout success rate post-deploy — rollback if conversion drops > 1%
Validate idempotent reservation keys prevent double-booking under timeout
goldBlastRadius: Longer saga timeout increases latency for checkout flow
Circuit breaker on Inventory Sync may temporarily block checkouts when tripped
Payment authorizations held longer before capture or void
Revenue impact if saga changes introduce new failure modes
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Commerce Team (owns both Cart Service and Checkout Orchestrator per art-cart-001). PIR-2026-031 (art-cart-005) is the motivating incident. Dependency map must show all saga participants. Payments Team coordination required per art-cart-002.

scenarios.title: Payment Gateway Security Patch Deployment
scenarios.prompt: InfoSec has flagged a critical vulnerability in the Payment Gateway's TLS configuration. A patch must be deployed within 24 hours. The Payment Gateway is PCI-DSS scoped. Determine the owner, dependencies, blast radius, and safe deployment procedure.
goldSafeActions: Obtain security review approval from InfoSec before deployment
Coordinate with Commerce Team (Checkout Orchestrator owner) for downstream impact
Verify TLS patch does not break ext-payment-processor connectivity
Deploy with zero-downtime rolling strategy (PCI requires no card data exposure during transition)
Monitor authorization success rate — rollback if auth failures > 0.1%
goldBlastRadius: All checkout transactions blocked if Payment Gateway goes down during patch
External payment processor connection may fail if TLS version mismatch
Revenue loss for duration of any outage
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Payments Team per art-cart-003. PCI compliance requires security review. Blast radius is all checkouts per art-cart-003. Must coordinate with Commerce Team per art-cart-002 (Payments Team sign-off requirement works bidirectionally).

scenarios.title: Deploy Updated Fraud Detection Model
scenarios.prompt: The Risk Engineering Team has a new fraud detection model ready after the PIR-2026-042 remediation. The model includes feature-freshness validation and requires canary scoring. Map the dependencies, assess blast radius, identify the owner, and plan safe deployment.
goldSafeActions: Run canary scoring on last 24h transactions — verify drift < 0.5% before promotion
Obtain Risk Engineering + Data Science sign-off for model deployment
Verify feature-freshness validation catches stale promotional pricing data
Monitor false-negative rate post-deploy — rollback if > baseline + 0.2%
Confirm Audit Logger is recording model version for compliance traceability
goldBlastRadius: False negatives lead to financial loss from approved fraudulent orders
False positives increase order rejection rate and customer friction
Checkout Orchestrator falls back to rule-based scoring if Fraud Detection unavailable (higher false-positive rate)
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Risk Engineering Team per art-risk-001. PIR-2026-042 (art-risk-005) provides the remediation requirements including canary scoring and feature-freshness validation. Blast radius extends to financial loss.

scenarios.title: Identity Provider Emergency Rotation
scenarios.prompt: Platform Security Team has detected a potential JWT signing key compromise. The Identity Provider signing keys must be rotated immediately. This affects all authenticated services. Determine the owner, map the full blast radius, and plan safe rotation steps.
goldSafeActions: Coordinate with SRE for off-peak deployment window (< 2 AM ET preferred)
Use dual-key rotation: add new key first, then retire old key after propagation
Verify all downstream services can validate tokens signed with new key
Monitor authentication failure rate across all services during rotation
Confirm Access Control policy engine accepts new token format
goldBlastRadius: All authenticated operations blocked if rotation is not seamless
Access Control loses fine-grained permissions — all services fall back to coarse allow/deny
Storefront, Mobile BFF, and Admin Portal users cannot log in during disruption
Platform-wide outage if IdP unavailability exceeds token refresh window
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Platform Security Team per art-risk-004. IdP is root of all authentication; blast radius is platform-wide per art-risk-004. Access Control dependency per art-risk-002 shows cascading impact to every authenticated service.
